hypothesis: If a two-stage transfer learning approach—pretraining EfficientNet-B0 on additive-free SEM images of NCM cathodes and then fine-tuning on additive-induced samples—is used, then the classifier will achieve higher class-wise accuracy and F1 scores on the nine-class NCM composition × aging-state dataset than classifiers trained from scratch or with a single training stage, because morphology-related SEM features learned in the source domain are transferable to the target domain.
